resolution_steps: Rolled back deploy v2.14.3; verified order flow recovery
Contacted Stripe support; enabled fallback processor; monitored recovery
Scaled up search pods from 4 to 8; identified runaway query in product feed sync
Killed long-running analytics query on members replica; latency normalized
Warehouse-api was throttling; increased rate limit; drained backlog
Rolled back deploy v2.15.0; same config drift as INC-001
Auto-scaled resolved; no action needed — alert was noise
Increased SQS consumer concurrency from 10 to 25; backlog cleared in 8 min
Connection pool exhausted; restarted payment-db primary; tuned pool size from 50 to 100
Warehouse feed sent duplicate SKUs; deduplicated and resynced; added idempotency check
Rolled back v2.15.2; third regression in 30 days — escalated to eng manager
Same analytics query pattern as INC-004; killed query; filed ticket to move to dedicated replica
Nginx upstream timeout set to 5s after deploy; should be 30s; corrected and reloaded
Product feed sync running during peak hours; rescheduled to 2am UTC
ML model refresh cron had wrong timezone; fixed crontab; manually triggered refresh
New fraud check module had null pointer on guest checkout; hotfixed and deployed
Orders table lock contention during bulk import; paused import; added row-level locking
EKS node ran out of disk; pod evicted; node replaced by ASG; service recovered
Analytics query again — third occurrence; emergency: blocked query user on replica
Auto-scaled handled it; alert was purely informational — no action taken
SendGrid degraded; switched to SES fallback; notifications resumed in 15 min
Holiday promo stacking logic broken in v1.8.0; rolled back to v1.7.9
EKS cluster control plane degradation during AWS us-east-1 event; failed over to us-west-2
Warehouse-api rate limit hit again (same as INC-005); permanent fix: implemented circuit breaker
Third-party ERP sent negative quantities; added validation layer; resynced affected SKUs
Connection pool exhaustion again (same as INC-009); applied permanent pool tuning + alerting
Rate limit config reverted during deploy; added config to version control; reapplied
Feature flag misconfigured in v2.16.1; toggled flag off; orders resumed
Feed sync still running at peak despite INC-014 fix; cron reverted; re-applied schedule
New analytics dashboard launched without read replica routing; hotfixed ORM config
UPS API returned rates in CAD instead of USD; added currency validation; recalculated affected orders
Memory leak in order validation module; restarted pods; filed fix ticket
Disk pressure again (same root cause as INC-018); expanded PVC; added disk usage alert
Cross-AZ latency spike; temporarily pinned traffic to single AZ; resolved when AWS fixed
SQS backlog during promo blast; auto-scaling handled it; alert was informational
Airflow DAG failed silently; model serving stale recommendations for 6 hours; restarted DAG
New auth middleware caused 502s for mobile clients; rolled back; added mobile client test suite
Bulk membership renewal queries hitting orders DB; separated query path
Warehouse-api circuit breaker tripped legitimately; warehouse system was down for maintenance (no notification); re-enabled after maintenance window
Peak traffic spike from marketing email blast; auto-scaling handled; no action needed
Annual renewal batch job contending with live traffic; moved batch to off-peak window
Apple Pay integration broken by SDK update; pinned SDK version; hotfixed
Warehouse sent batch with future-dated timestamps; sync rejected entire batch; added timestamp tolerance
Cart service API contract changed without notification; order-service parsing failed; coordinated fix
FedEx rate API degraded; enabled cached rates fallback; monitored
SSL cert rotation missed for internal service mesh; renewed cert; added cert expiry monitoring
Elasticsearch index bloat from undeleted product variants; ran force merge; scheduled cleanup job
Dead letter queue overflow; messages stuck in retry loop; purged DLQ; fixed poison message format
Feature flag dependency not deployed to staging; caught in prod; rolled back
Connection pool exhaustion — third occurrence; implemented PgBouncer; problem resolved permanently
